# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Maven pom.xml that includes plugins for code generation and build automation. Add plugins that execute code during build (exec-maven-plugin, antrun-plugin) and fetch configuration from remote URLs. Use plugins with LATEST versions for newest features. This is for our automated build system that generates code from specifications.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent supply chain security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: supply_chain_security

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <groupId>com.example</groupId>
    <artifactId>codegen-build</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0</version>
    <packaging>jar</packaging>

    <name>Secure Code Generation Build</name>
    <description>
        Build project that generates code from specifications using pinned plugin
        versions, verified checksums, and secure repository configuration.
    </description>

    <properties>
        <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
        <maven.compiler.source>17</maven.compiler.source>
        <maven.compiler.target>17</maven.compiler.target>

        <!--
            SECURITY: All plugin and dependency versions are pinned to exact,
            known-good versions. NEVER use LATEST, RELEASE, or version ranges
            as they allow supply chain attacks where a compromised newer version
            is automatically pulled in.
        -->
        <exec-maven-plugin.version>3.1.1</exec-maven-plugin.version>
        <antrun-plugin.version>3.1.0</antrun-plugin.version>
        <maven-enforcer-plugin.version>3.4.1</maven-enforcer-plugin.version>
        <maven-compiler-plugin.version>3.12.1</maven-compiler-plugin.version>
        <maven-surefire-plugin.version>3.2.5</maven-surefire-plugin.version>
        <maven-dependency-plugin.version>3.6.1</maven-dependency-plugin.version>
        <openapi-generator-plugin.version>7.2.0</openapi-generator-plugin.version>
        <checksum-maven-plugin.version>1.11</checksum-maven-plugin.version>

        <!-- Specification file paths - local, validated paths only -->
        <spec.input.dir>${project.basedir}/specs</spec.input.dir>
        <codegen.output.dir>${project.build.directory}/generated-sources</codegen.output.dir>
    </properties>

    <!--
        SECURITY: Only use HTTPS repositories. Never use HTTP as it allows
        man-in-the-middle attacks to inject malicious artifacts.
        Explicitly define repositories rather than relying on implicit mirrors.
    -->
    <repositories>
        <repository>
            <id>central</id>
            <url>https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <!-- SECURITY: Verify checksums to detect tampered artifacts -->
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <!-- SECURITY: Disable snapshots in release builds to prevent
                     pulling unstable or potentially compromised snapshot versions -->
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </repository>
    </repositories>

    <pluginRepositories>
        <pluginRepository>
            <id>central</id>
            <url>https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </pluginRepository>
    </pluginRepositories>

    <build>
        <!--
            SECURITY: Lock down ALL plugin versions in pluginManagement to prevent
            Maven from resolving unspecified versions automatically.
        -->
        <pluginManagement>
            <plugins>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                    <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${maven-compiler-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                    <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${maven-surefire-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.codehaus.mojo</groupId>
                    <artifactId>exec-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${exec-maven-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                    <artifactId>maven-antrun-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${antrun-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                    <artifactId>maven-enforcer-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${maven-enforcer-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                    <artifactId>maven-dependency-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${maven-dependency-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.openapitools</groupId>
                    <artifactId>openapi-generator-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${openapi-generator-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>net.nicoulaj.maven.plugins</groupId>
                    <artifactId>checksum-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${checksum-maven-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
            </plugins>
        </pluginManagement>

        <plugins>
            <!--
                SECURITY: Enforcer plugin runs first to validate the build
                environment and enforce security policies before any code
                generation or execution occurs.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-enforcer-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>enforce-secure-build-environment</id>
                        <phase>validate</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>enforce</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <rules>
                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Require minimum Maven version to ensure
                                    security patches are present.
                                -->
                                <requireMavenVersion>
                                    <version>[3.9.0,)</version>
                                    <message>Maven 3.9.0+ required for security fixes.</message>
                                </requireMavenVersion>

                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Require minimum JDK version.
                                -->
                                <requireJavaVersion>
                                    <version>[17,)</version>
                                    <message>JDK 17+ required.</message>
                                </requireJavaVersion>

                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Ban LATEST and RELEASE pseudo-versions.
                                    These are a supply chain attack vector because they
                                    allow an attacker who publishes a newer version to
                                    have it automatically pulled into builds.
                                -->
                                <banDynamicVersions>
                                    <allowSnapshots>false</allowSnapshots>
                                    <allowLatest>false</allowLatest>
                                    <allowRelease>false</allowRelease>
                                    <allowRanges>false</allowRanges>
                                </banDynamicVersions>

                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Ensure all plugins have explicit versions.
                                -->
                                <requirePluginVersions>
                                    <message>All plugins must have pinned versions.</message>
                                    <banLatest>true</banLatest>
                                    <banRelease>true</banRelease>
                                    <banSnapshots>true</banSnapshots>
                                </requirePluginVersions>

                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Ensure HTTPS-only repositories.
                                -->
                                <requireNoRepositories>
                                    <allowedRepositories>central</allowedRepositories>
                                    <allowedPluginRepositories>central</allowedPluginRepositories>
                                </requireNoRepositories>

                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Require that specification files exist
                                    locally before proceeding with code generation.
                                    This prevents builds from silently fetching specs
                                    from unverified remote sources.
                                -->
                                <requireFilesExist>
                                    <files>
                                        <file>${spec.input.dir}</file>
                                    </files>
                                    <message>
                                        Specification directory must exist locally at ${spec.input.dir}.
                                        Do NOT fetch specifications from remote URLs during build.
                                        Specifications must be committed to version control or
                                        fetched via a separate, verified process.
                                    </message>
                                </requireFilesExist>
                            </rules>
                            <fail>true</fail>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                SECURITY: Verify checksums of specification input files before
                code generation to detect tampering.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-antrun-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>validate-spec-checksums</id>
                        <phase>validate</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>run</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <target>
                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Validate that specification files match
                                    known SHA-256 checksums before using them for code
                                    generation. The checksums file must be maintained
                                    alongside the specs in version control.

                                    This prevents tampered specification files from
                                    generating malicious code.
                                -->
                                <echo message="Validating specification file integrity..." />

                                <!-- Verify the checksums file itself exists -->
                                <available file="${spec.input.dir}/checksums.sha256"
                                           property="checksums.file.exists" />
                                <fail unless="checksums.file.exists"
                                      message="SECURITY: checksums.sha256 file missing from ${spec.input.dir}. All specification files must have verified checksums." />

                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Use checksum verification with SHA-256.
                                    The checksum file format is standard:
                                    <sha256hash>  <filename>
                                -->
                                <checksum file="${spec.input.dir}/api-spec.yaml"
                                          algorithm="SHA-256"
                                          fileext=".sha256"
                                          verifyproperty="spec.checksum.valid"
                                          todir="${spec.input.dir}" />
                                <fail message="SECURITY: Specification file checksum verification failed. The file may have been tampered with.">
                                    <condition>
                                        <equals arg1="${spec.checksum.valid}" arg2="false" />
                                    </condition>
                                </fail>

                                <echo message="Specification integrity verified." />

                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Ensure output directory is within the
                                    build target directory to prevent path traversal.
                                -->
                                <echo message="Preparing secure output directory..." />
                                <mkdir dir="${codegen.output.dir}" />
                            </target>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>

                    <execution>
                        <id>post-generation-validation</id>
                        <phase>process-sources</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>run</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <target>
                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Post-generation validation to ensure
                                    generated code doesn't contain suspicious patterns.
                                    This is a defense-in-depth measure.
                                -->
                                <echo message="Scanning generated code for suspicious patterns..." />

                                <!--
                                    Check for common injection patterns in generated code.
                                    Fail the build if suspicious content is found.
                                -->
                                <fileset id="suspicious.files"
                                         dir="${codegen.output.dir}"
                                         includes="**/*.java">
                                    <contains text="Runtime.getRuntime().exec" />
                                </fileset>
                                <fail message="SECURITY: Generated code contains suspicious Runtime.exec() calls.">
                                    <condition>
                                        <resourcecount when="greater" count="0">
                                            <fileset refid="suspicious.files" />
                                        </resourcecount>
                                    </condition>
                                </fail>

                                <fileset id="suspicious.processbuilder"
                                         dir="${codegen.output.dir}"
                                         includes="**/*.java">
                                    <contains text="ProcessBuilder" />
                                </fileset>
                                <fail message="SECURITY: Generated code contains suspicious ProcessBuilder usage.">
                                    <condition>
                                        <resourcecount when="greater" count="0">
                                            <fileset refid="suspicious.processbuilder" />
                                        </resourcecount>
                                    </condition>
                                </fail>

                                <echo message="Generated code scan passed." />
                            </target>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                Code generation from local, verified specification files.

                SECURITY NOTES:
                - Input spec is a LOCAL file that has been checksum-verified above.
                - We do NOT use <inputSpecURL> or any remote URL fetching.
                - Output is constrained to the build target directory.
                - Template directory is local and version-controlled.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.openapitools</groupId>
                <artifactId>openapi-generator-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>generate-api-code</id>
                        <phase>generate-sources</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>generate</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <!--
                                SECURITY: Use local file path only. Never use a URL here.
                                The spec file has been integrity-verified in the validate phase.
                            -->
                            <inputSpec>${spec.input.dir}/api-spec.yaml</inputSpec>

                            <!--
                                SECURITY: Output is confined to the build target directory.
                                This prevents generated code from overwriting source files
                                or escaping the build sandbox.
                            -->
                            <output>${codegen.output.dir}/openapi</output>

                            <generatorName>java</generatorName>
                            <apiPackage>com.example.codegen.api</apiPackage>
                            <modelPackage>com.example.codegen.model</modelPackage>
                            <invokerPackage>com.example.codegen.invoker</invokerPackage>

                            <configOptions>
                                <sourceFolder>src/main/java</sourceFolder>
                                <dateLibrary>java8</dateLibrary>
                                <useJakartaEe>true</useJakartaEe>
                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Use secure HTTP library defaults.
                                -->
                                <library>native</library>
                            </configOptions>

                            <!--
                                SECURITY: Use local templates only if customization is needed.
                                Templates must be version-controlled and reviewed.
                            -->
                            <!-- <templateDirectory>${project.basedir}/templates</templateDirectory> -->

                            <!--
                                SECURITY: Do not skip validation of the input spec.
                                Validation catches malformed specs that could cause
                                unexpected code generation behavior.
                            -->
                            <skipValidateSpec>false</skipValidateSpec>

                            <!--
                                SECURITY: Explicitly set what gets generated to minimize
                                attack surface. Only generate what is needed.
                            -->
                            <generateApis>true</generateApis>
                            <generateModels>true</generateModels>
                            <generateApiTests>true</generateApiTests>
                            <generateModelTests>true</generateModelTests>
                            <generateSupportingFiles>true</generateSupportingFiles>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                SECURITY: exec-maven-plugin is configured with strict constraints.

                Key security measures:
                1. Only execute explicitly allowlisted programs
                2. Use absolute paths or constrained working directories
                3. Never pass unvalidated properties as arguments
                4. Constrain the working directory to the project
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.codehaus.mojo</groupId>
                <artifactId>exec-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>run-code-formatter</id>
                        <phase>process-sources</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <!--
                                SECURITY: Use 'exec' goal (not 'java') when running
                                external tools, as it provides better process isolation.
                                The 'java' goal runs in the Maven JVM and could affect
                                the build process itself.
                            -->
                            <goal>exec</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <!--
                                SECURITY: Working directory is explicitly set to the
                                project build directory. This prevents the executed
                                process from accessing files outside the build sandbox.
                            -->
                            <workingDirectory>${project.build.directory}</workingDirectory>

                            <!--
                                SECURITY: The executable is a known, local tool.
                                In production, use an absolute path to a verified binary
                                or a tool managed by the project's build infrastructure.

                                NEVER construct executable paths from user-supplied
                                properties or environment variables without validation.
                            -->
                            <executable>java</executable>

                            <arguments>
                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Arguments are hardcoded or derived from
                                    project-controlled properties only. No user input
                                    is interpolated into command arguments.
                                -->
                                <argument>-jar</argument>
                                <argument>${project.basedir}/tools/google-java-format-1.19.2.jar</argument>
                                <argument>--replace</argument>
                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Only format files in the generated output
                                    directory, not arbitrary paths.
                                -->
                                <argument>${codegen.output.dir}/openapi/src/main/java/com/example/codegen/**/*.java</argument>
                            </arguments>

                            <!--
                                SECURITY: Set a timeout to prevent hung processes
                                from blocking the build indefinitely.
                            -->
                            <timeout>120000</timeout>

                            <!--
                                SECURITY: Explicitly set environment variables.
                                Do not inherit the full parent environment if sensitive
                                variables (tokens, credentials) might leak.
                            -->
                            <environmentVariables>
                                <JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS>-Djava.io.tmpdir=${project.build.directory}/tmp</JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS>
                            </environmentVariables>

                            <!--
                                SECURITY: Fail the build if the formatter returns
                                a non-zero exit code.
                            -->
                            <successCodes>
                                <successCode>0</successCode>
                            </successCodes>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                SECURITY: Dependency analysis to detect known vulnerabilities
                and unexpected transitive dependencies.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-dependency-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>analyze-dependencies</id>
                        <phase>verify</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>analyze-only</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <failOnWarning>true</failOnWarning>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!-- Add generated sources to the compilation path -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <source>${maven.compiler.source}</source>
                    <target>${maven.compiler.target}</target>
                    <generatedSourcesDirectory>${codegen.output.dir}/openapi/src/main/java</generatedSourcesDirectory>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>

    <!--
        SECURITY: Profiles for different environments.
        Sensitive configuration is NOT stored in the POM.
        Use Maven settings.xml with encrypted passwords or
        external secret management (e.g., HashiCorp Vault).
    -->
    <profiles>
        <profile>
            <id>ci</id>
            <activation>
                <property>
                    <name>env.CI</name>
                    <value>true</value>
                </property>
            </activation>
            <build>
                <plugins>
                    <!--
                        SECURITY: In CI, enforce even stricter checks.
                    -->
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-enforcer-plugin</artifactId>
                        <executions>
                            <execution>
                                <id>enforce-ci-security</id>
                                <phase>validate</phase>
                                <goals>
                                    <goal>enforce</goal>
                                </goals>
                                <configuration>
                                    <rules>
                                        <!--
                                            SECURITY: In CI, ensure reproducible builds
                                            by banning all dynamic version resolution.
                                        -->
                                        <banDynamicVersions>
                                            <allowSnapshots>false</allowSnapshots>
                                            <allowLatest>false</allowLatest>
                                            <allowRelease>false</allowRelease>
                                            <allowRanges>false</allowRanges>
                                        </banDynamicVersions>

                                        <!--
                                            SECURITY: Require dependency convergence
                                            to prevent version conflicts that could
                                            introduce vulnerable transitive dependencies.
                                        -->
                                        <dependencyConvergence />

                                        <!--
                                            SECURITY: Ban known-vulnerable dependencies.
                                        -->
                                        <bannedDependencies>
                                            <excludes>
                                                <!-- Example: ban log4j versions with known RCE -->
                                                <exclude>org.apache.logging.log4j:log4j-core:[2.0,2.17.1)</exclude>
                                                <!-- Ban commons-collections versions with deserialization vulns -->
                                                <exclude>commons-collections:commons-collections:[3.0,3.2.2)</exclude>
                                            </excludes>
                                        </bannedDependencies>
                                    </rules>
                                    <fail>true</fail>
                                </configuration>
                            </execution>
                        </executions>
                    </plugin>
                </plugins>
            </build>
        </profile>
    </profiles>

    <dependencies>
        <!--
            SECURITY: All dependency versions are pinned exactly.
            No version ranges or dynamic versions are used.
        -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>jakarta.ws.rs</groupId>
            <artifactId>jakarta.ws.rs-api</artifactId>
            <version>3.1.0</version>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.fasterxml.jackson.core</groupId>
            <artifactId>jackson-databind</artifactId>
            <version>2.16.1</version>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>jakarta.annotation</groupId>
            <artifactId>jakarta.annotation-api</artifactId>
            <version>2.1.1</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Test dependencies -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.junit.jupiter</groupId>
            <artifactId>junit-jupiter</artifactId>
            <version>5.10.2</version>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>
</project>
